creator: destination_wellknown
caption:
Look, I’m not trying to badmouth any particular establishment, but a New Yorker wouldn’t eat in Times Square and a Londoner would probably not meet their mates for dinner around Oxford Street 😇

In the same way, it’s probably hard to find a Copenhagener with a favourite restaurant in Nyhavn 🤓

But as I try to be a positive travel blogger, here are a few nice places around Nyhavn: 

📍Broens Gadekøkken 😛
📍Café Mønten (cosy little bar) 
📍Gnam Gnam (great pasta)
📍Aamanns Replik (excellent smørrebrød, but pricey)
📍Nebbiolo Winebar (the name doesn’t lie, it’s a wine bar) 

For more Copenhagen things to do, check out my new Youtube video where I share 20 of my favourite things to do in the Danish capital 🇩🇰

#copenhagen #copenhagenfood #nyhavn

geo_locations: Nebbiolo Winebar (the name doesn’t lie, it’s a wine bar)
pinned_places: Nebbiolo Winebar (the name doesn’t lie, it’s a wine bar)

transcription.text: Don't make this mistake in Copenhagen. 99% of people who travel to Copenhagen will head to this beautiful part of town called Nyhavn. And that's definitely not a mistake, because Nyhavn is well worth a visit. But it's also the most touristy part of town, and with that typically comes overpriced restaurants with pretty bad food. So when hunger strikes, instead of eating in Nyhavn, you should cross this bridge over to a little street food market where you can get everything from Danish hot dogs to the world famous gasoline grilled burger.
